- [Report this comment](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsatyanadella_counting-down-to-a-new-build-in-san-francisco-activity-7434688896669560833-PqKW&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

Strategic platform moments like this often signal more than product updates — they reflect ecosystem intent and capital allocation priorities.

At [Microsoft](https://www.linkedin.com/company/microsoft?trk=public_post_comment-text), the countdown to the next Build in San Francisco reinforces how deeply the company is embedding AI across its cloud, developer, and enterprise stack.

[Satya Nadella](https://www.linkedin.com/in/satyanadella?trk=public_post_comment-text), your consistent focus on aligning platform innovation with real enterprise productivity continues to distinguish Microsoft’s execution discipline in a highly competitive AI cycle.

What stands out is the structural advantage of combining Azure scale, developer community depth, and responsible AI governance into one integrated flywheel.

In a market where many players are chasing capabilities, [Microsoft](https://www.linkedin.com/company/microsoft?trk=public_post_comment-text) is compounding ecosystem strength — a materially different long-term strategy.

As generative AI transitions from experimentation to enterprise-grade deployment, durable value will increasingly favor platforms that balance innovation velocity with trust, security, and commercial clarity.

Looking forward to how Build further strengthens [Microsoft](https://www.linkedin.com/company/microsoft?trk=public_post_comment-text)’s competitive positioning.

#Leadership #GlobalStrategy #Innovation #ArtificialIntelligence #CapitalMarkets #EnterpriseTechnology #DigitalTransformation #LongTermValue

Hyperscalers still measure growth in terabytes.

This visualization shows growth measured in overlap density — where new data adds structure, not storage.

If your storage model doesn’t distinguish physical space from logical space, it’s already obsolete. — Cecil A. Lacy Inventor of the Fragmental Overlap Storage System (FOSS) & Fragmental Network Protocol (FNP) Patent Pending: 19/264,676

- [Report this comment](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fsatyanadella_counting-down-to-a-new-build-in-san-francisco-activity-7434688896669560833-PqKW&trk=public_post_comment_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=COMMENT&_f=guest-reporting)

[Satya Nadella](https://www.linkedin.com/in/satyanadella?trk=public_post_comment-text) there's a lot riding on it for Microsoft's future. Windows 11 has completely removed a lot of control from the user and you've made arbitrary decisions. You've taken control away from the end user and administrator and that is unacceptable. In Windows 12 you better return all the control back to the end user and administrator. Not everyone wants to use your AI stuff. Otherwise, you're going to have a massive amount of people move away from your operating system somewhere else.

But I've heard the will follow him. Maybe released:

🚀 Windows 12 ( "Agentic OS"): Rumors point to a June launch in San Francisco. We’re moving beyond chat; OS will likely empower AI agents to execute tasks across apps autonomously (e.g., managing files/data without manual input). 🛡️ Windows Resiliency: Expect a major shift in security architecture. Microsoft is moving third-party security tools out of the kernel to prevent system-wide crashes (no more BSOD outages!). 💼 M365 E7 Tier: A potential new subscription for "Agent 365" to help enterprises manage and govern their "digital workers" alongside human employees. 🛠️ Azure AI Foundry: New tools for developers to build multi-agent "swarms" that collaborate on complex workflows.

Satya, events like Build always feel like a snapshot of where the industry is heading next. The pace of change in AI, cloud infrastructure, and developer tooling right now is incredible, and Microsoft has clearly positioned itself at the center of that conversation. What I always find interesting about these moments is how they shape the next wave of builders, since new tools lower the barrier for people to create, experiment, and ship faster than ever. Also, for anyone in my network who’s job searching while trying to keep up with all this change, my friends at [ApplyAll.co](http://ApplyAll.co?trk=public_post_comment-text) can help by guaranteeing interviews (or your money back) so you’re not stuck in application limbo. Good luck to everyone building and navigating the market right now.
